Fun trivia : Did you know that a particular woman was the last person that Jose Rizal bade his  personal goodbye?
In fact, he even named her in his famous last poem...
That woman's name was Huling...
Translated loosely in Filipino language from his famous last poem,  Mi ultimo adios ( My Last Farewell ), it reads, Huling, Paalam!
😅✌️😂✌️🤣✌️

Sometime in the late 90's, my family (headed by my late father) visited Fort Santiago. After circling the Fort, everyone, even our young children, agreed to follow the metallic footsteps of Rizal embedded along the way from Fort Santiago to the Luneta Park where his monument stands. It was a different kind of experience which our children still cherish today. ( I am not sure if the metallic footsteps are still there)

Just sharing po how it feels when we step or stand in places our national hero once trodden. It gives me a great sense of  being a Filipino.
Pwede po bang tagalog na lang para maintindihan ng masa

Such eloquence in your composition Sir about the last 24-hours of Jose Rizal, I could vividly imagine the events unfolding with the train of words, indeed let us not forget the lessons, sacrifices of our national hero, if only our politicians could digest such love for our country, how many more Rizals would it take pa to realize that the Filipinos are really worth dying for

The brilliant and multi talented Dr Jose Rizal always has his way. He even directed his path to martyrdom by making sure he was properly dressed for that very special occasion.

His brilliance was recognized when he was still alive in Europe and in death, pronounced prominently by Prime Minister Mahathir Mohammad of Malaysia who considered him as the Pride of the Malay race and a pioneer of Asian nationalism that could have probably influenced the minds of Mahatma Gandhi and Sun Yat Sen.

Today, we commemorate his martyrdom. But, are we worthy of his sacrifice? Was his death a catalyst for our collective betterment or was his martyrdom rendered useless as we are heading to our own martyrdoom.

In spite of all his accomplishments, tributes, monuments, parks and streets in his honor. José Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda is not yet legally honored as our national hero.

Ouchie. Great question that resonates deeply - are we really aware of what we reveal when we open our mouth. 

I believe in most cases, we are not, I am not. Because it's easier to be reactive, bias, and emotional. Perhaps it's also cultural for us as we often decide or judge base on our "pakiramdam" or "saloobin." The "loob" is our core as what our own cultural anthropologist or sociologist would claim. And that what we say or do would always have a "hugot" o "pinanggagalingan" na "saloobin". So yes, our culture na "kinalakihan" plus perhaps our folk Christianity often dictates our saloobin more than our critical and analytical self would reveal. 

This is not to make an excuse of who we are and where we're coming from culturally. But to resonate with this reflection with my cultural cap on which also goes well with self assessment, I guess. 

Thank you for sharing this good piece which is essential in confronting a "feeling" and perhaps "less thinking / critical" culture such as ours. I also am reminded to be very mindful as much as critical, to be kind as much reasonable. Parang magandang new year's resolution din ito. 🫡😍

At yung dead-end vs powerful questions? Gold. Instead of "Bakit ang gulo nito?" (na shuts down thinking), better yung "What am I missing here?" para mag-open up ng deeper discussion.

This also represents how different people perceive information the first time. Data meant to be ingested first, analyzed, scrutinized, cleaned up before presented to your audience or stakeholders. Information cannot be biased as sometimes this might implicate minority groups, only facts should remain and that itself would still warrants open interpretation. If you worked in tech or data science to be specific you can relate more as this is the typical ETL journey that the data is going through. Research is also another thing. That’s why people that are known to be insightful and thriving in their fields are not judgmental and reactive the first time. They follow a systematic approach and cannot be swayed by emotions. There might be outliers but in general they aren’t. Because nothing is 100% certain, only persuasive data to guide decisions is there.

A mom driving kids or going to the market.  But since they dont require drivers ed, there is a lack of discipline and a question of what rules do apply.

It is with great sadness that we do not possess the art and science of governance.

The proliferation of cars either combustion or EV powered, motorcycles, EV trikes came about due to one main issue. 

Transportation .

The need to move people or goods from one point to another could not be provided by the government. Thus, the need to satisfy immediately such sentiments became necessary. 

Unfortunately, like most government initiated programs, the lack of a comprehensive study on its  negative effects are studied ones we have experienced it. 

The liberalization of the importation of electric vehicles immediately solved the transport issue. However, like hydra, it created a lot of issues, such as,

1. Accidents
     How can we penalize an e trike drivers if they are not regulated since they are not required to have a license?
Who will pay for the damages since they are not insured?

2. Additional reckless drivers
We have witnessed accidents involving reckless or kamote drivers involved in preventable situations. Here we are, adding additional road accidents.

Further, I have seen very young and senior citizens weaving in and out of their lanes without any turning signs. Another entitled sector.

3. Infrastructure 
Prior to these e-trike and motorcycle era, we have already experienced lack of roads. Instead of addressing such issue, they even reduced a car’ mobility by reducing the roads by allocating a special lane for bikes. By the way, this is a correct program but wrong implementation. A 3 lane road becomes a 2 lane road. The 3rd lane is spliced into 2. One for bikes and the other one becomes non usable since a car can’t decently use it unless he do a James Bond, by driving only two wheels, in front and back while the other wheels are suspended in mid air.

Unfortunately, we have a penchant for solving an immediate need and let the next administration address any consequential problems and blame them for bad governance.

Our political leaders have a myopic view of governance wherein it is so focused on their present administration wherein accolades and praises should be confined while they are in power. 

Can the government still curtail the excesses of the e- trike industry? 

The question is, can we still return the squeezed toothpaste back to its tube?

I don't have a problem with e-bikes, e-trikes, and the 4-wheeled ones that look like golf carts. The problem is the one driving it. Kasi mga minors na driver, pati mga matanda na, pasingit-singit sa mga sasakyan, mga ang bibilis magpatakbo kahit may nakitang tatawid, mga sumasampa sa sidewalk tapos magagalit kasi ang bagal nyo maglakad hindi sya makadaan, saka yung nasa gitna sya ng daan pero mabagal andar nya at ayaw gumilid kahit bumubusina ka na kaya may parang prusisyon ng mga kotse sa likod nya. Kahit bigyan na lang ng discount sa pagkuha ng license pag e-bike etc. ang hawak, basta para sa akin dapat may license pag may motor at ginagamit sa public roads along with other vehicles.

The break up of the Uniteam was an opportunity to prove the point, as competing pay masters demand different survey results, the unanimous surveys pre 2022 election will be broken up.  That is exactly what happened in the 2025 election surveys, Publicus stood outside while everyone else stuck to the plan (the admins).
